revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Stundenkalender des Ramses II.]] [[File:Egypt.Ramesseum.02.jpg|thumb|The Ramesseum]]
The '''Hour Calendar of [[Ramesses II.]]''' lists the corresponding division of day and night hours and dates back to his ninth regnal year (1271–1270 BCE). The {{ill|Papyrus|en|Papyrus|qid=Q125576|lt=Papyrus|1=Papyrus}} containing the hour records, now housed in the [[Egyptian Museum (Cairo)|Egyptian Museum in Cairo]], was discovered by {{ill|Archaeologist|en|Archaeologist|qid=Q3621491|lt=Archaeologist|1=Archaeologist}}s in the {{ill|Ramesseum|en|Ramesseum|qid=Q311581|lt=Ramesseum|1=Ramesseum}} in {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|en|Thebes, Egypt|qid=Q101583|lt=Thebes-West|1=Thebes, Egypt}}.
== Background == === Month Names ===
{{ill|Alan Gardiner|en|Alan Gardiner|qid=Q441060|lt=Alan Gardiner|1=Alan Gardiner}} and [[Richard-Anthony Parker]] suggest that months shifted by one month over the course of Egyptian calendar history, which led to the [[Egyptian New Year|New Year Festival]]—originally the first month of [[Wepet-renpet]]—falling on the twelfth month under {{ill|Amenhotep I|en|Amenhotep I|qid=Q157966|lt=Amenhotep I|1=Amenhotep I}}.
The names of months were linked to key festivals, presenting an idealized version of the calendar that began with the [[heliacal rising]] of the {{ill|Star|en|Star|qid=Q523|lt=Star|1=Star}} {{ill|Sirius|en|Sirius|qid=Q3409|lt=Sirius|1=Sirius}} (embodied by the goddess {{ill|Sopdet|en|Sopdet|qid=Q930167|lt=Sopdet|1=Sopdet}}). The shift in {{ill|Season|en|Season|qid=Q24384|lt=Season|1=Season}}s in the [[:en:Ancient Egyptian calendar|administrative calendar]] is well-documented through the year calendar of Ramesses II.
The festivals tied to the months were similarly idealized and did not always correspond with the administrative calendar, as many ceremonies adhered to the [[Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lunar calendar]], which operated independently of the standard administrative calendar.
=== Division of Hours ===
The division of hours does not align with the actual [[Day in ancient Egypt|day and night lengths]] in ancient Egypt, as average [[:en:day length|day length]] ranged from 10 h 21 min (December 21/22) to 13 h 44 min (June 21/22). Including morning and evening {{ill|Twilight|en|Twilight|qid=Q164160|lt=Twilight|1=Twilight}}—each lasting roughly two hours—the extremes ranged from six to 18 hours.
[[:en:Egyptologists|Egyptologists]] like [[Siegfried Schott]], Richard-Anthony Parker, [[Alexandra von Lieven]], and {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}} established direct connections between this hour division and the [[Hour division in ancient Egypt|ancient Egyptian division of hours]], derived from entries on [[:en:Diagonal star clock|Diagonal star clock]]s and [[:en:shadow clock|shadow clock]]s. Further links are found in the [[Book of the Night]], [[Book of Day]], [[Nutbook]], and texts on [[hour gods]].
{| class="wikitable" style="width: 100%; border: 2px solid gray;" |- {{Ägyptologie TblHighlight}} ! style="width:15%"| Month ! style="width:20%"| Season ! style="width:18%"| Day Hours ! style="width:18%"| Night Hours ! style="width:29%"| Month Name |- | {{ill|Thoth|en|Thoth|qid=Q146921|lt=Thoth|1=Thoth}} | {{ill|July|en|July|qid=Q121|lt=July|1=July}} to {{ill|August|en|August|qid=Q122|lt=August|1=August}} | 16 hours | 8 hours | Trunkenheit |- | {{ill|Paopi|en|Paopi|qid=Q731505|lt=Paopi|1=Paopi}} | August to {{ill|September|en|September|qid=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} | 14 hours | 10 hours | {{ill|Opet Festival|en|Opet Festival|qid=Q1194158|lt=Opet Festival|1=Opet Festival}} |- | {{ill|Hathor|en|Hathor|qid=Q133343|lt=Hathor|1=Hathor}} | September to {{ill|October|en|October|qid=Q124|lt=October|1=October}} | 12 hours | 12 hours | [[Sokar Festival]] |- | {{ill|Koiak|en|Koiak|qid=Q1410257|lt=Koiak|1=Koiak}} | October to {{ill|November|en|November|qid=Q125|lt=November|1=November}} | 10 hours | 14 hours | [[Osiris Festival]] |- | {{ill|Tobi (month)|en|Tobi (month)|qid=Q290480|lt=Tobi (month)|1=Tobi (month)}} | November to {{ill|December|en|December|qid=Q126|lt=December|1=December}} | 8 hours | 16 hours | [[Offering festival|Month of Offering]] |- | [[:en:Mechir|Mechir]] | December to {{ill|January|en|January|qid=Q108|lt=January|1=January}} | 6 hours | 18 hours | [[Mechir Festival]] |- | {{ill|Paremhat|en|Paremhat|qid=Q546893|lt=Paremhat|1=Paremhat}} | January to {{ill|February|en|February|qid=Q109|lt=February|1=February}} | 8 hours | 16 hours | [[Amenophis Festivals=Festival dates|Great Festival of King Amenhotep I]] |- | [[Pa-en-Renenutet]] | February to {{ill|March|en|March|qid=Q110|lt=March|1=March}} | 10 hours | 14 hours | [[Renenutet Festival]] |- | [[Pa-en-Khonsu]] | March to {{ill|April|en|April|qid=Q118|lt=April|1=April}} | 12 hours | 12 hours | [[Khonsu Festival]] |- | [[Pa-en-Inet]] | April to {{ill|May|en|May|qid=Q119|lt=May|1=May}} | 14 hours | 10 hours | {{ill|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|en|Beautiful Festival of the Valley|qid=Q478205|lt=Beautiful Festival of the Valley|1=Beautiful Festival of the Valley}} |- | {{ill|Epip|en|Epip|qid=Q1672224|lt=Epip|1=Epip}} | May to {{ill|June|en|June|qid=Q120|lt=June|1=June}} | 16 hours | 8 hours | [[Ipip Festival]] |- | {{ill|Mesori|en|Mesori|qid=Q1621298|lt=Mesori|1=Mesori}} | June to July | 18 hours | 6 hours | [[Egyptian New Year|New Year's Festival]] |}
== Literature ==
* Jaroslav Černý: ''Catalogue des ostraca hiératiques non littéraires de Deir el-Médinéh''. Institut Français d’Archéologie Orientale, Cairo 1935. * Christian Leitz: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren.'' Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9. * Christian Leitz: ''Studien zur ägyptischen Astronomie.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1991, ISBN 3-447-03157-3. * Richard-Anthony Parker: ''The calendars of ancient Egypt.'' Chicago Press, Chicago 1950. * Siegfried Schott: ''Altägyptische Festdaten.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz/ Wiesbaden 1950. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5. {{translated page|de|Stundenkalender des Ramses II.|version=217932765|comment=Imported full de history}}
[[de:Stundenkalender des Ramses II.]]
